Title: 10 Best no-code SaaS Builders You Can’t Miss In 2024
Author: team-epyc
Content:
The SaaS market is on fire, set to soar to 195 billion dollars by 2024, and let us tell you, that's not just pocket change. 

It's a clear sign of the times: flexibility and speed aren't just nice-to-haves; they're the very pillars of the modern software industry. 

And the hero of this story? No-code SaaS builders.

Think of them as the Swiss Army knife for digital innovation. No-code builders are opening doors for those with big ideas but without the "speak in code" skillset.

So what's the big deal with these no-code wonders, you might ask? It's simple. 

They're chipping away at the old barriers, the ones that used to say, "No technical know-how? No software for you!" Not anymore. 

Today, if you can think of it, there's a good chance you can build it, all thanks to the drag-and-drop ease of these platforms.

In the next few scrolls, we're diving deep into the world of no-code SaaS builders. We'll spotlight the best of the bunch, those who are setting the standard, making waves, and turning heads in the industry.

Some of our Favourite No-code SaaS Builders

Webflow 

This visual builder is a haven for designers who aim to create more than just functional websites; they craft digital masterpieces. 

With Webflow, the 'wow' factor is just the beginning. It’s the tool of choice for those who care about aesthetics as much as performance, ensuring that your website stands out in design and functionality.

Imagine having the freedom to construct a website with the finesse of a seasoned developer and the eye of a designer without writing a single line of code. 

That’s the reality with Webflow. You’re in control, dragging and dropping components to build your pages. It's like playing with building blocks, except these blocks can form anything from a sleek blog to a full-fledged eCommerce store.

But Webflow isn’t just about looks; it’s also about brains. It's SEO-optimized out of the box, which means your site isn’t just a pretty face in the digital world; it’s also set up to rank well on Google. This is crucial because what good is a stunning website if no one can find it? 

Webflow ensures that your site is:

  • Discoverable, 

  • Readable, and 

  • Favored by search engines, 

Which can be a game-changer for your SaaS offering.

Now, let's talk about dynamism and responsiveness, two must-haves in today’s web design. Webflow doesn’t disappoint. 

Whether your audience visits your site on a smartphone, tablet, or desktop, your Webflow site is built to look great and function seamlessly. 

The platform offers a full range of animations and interactions, allowing you to create a truly interactive experience. 

It’s about bringing your site to life, engaging visitors with motion and action that feels smooth and natural, with no jerky transitions or awkward pauses.

Webflow is for the SaaS website builder who wants to dive into the creative process headfirst without getting bogged down by the backend. 

It's for those who want to push the envelope on what a website can do and how it can look, all while ensuring that the technical SEO groundwork is solid. 

With Webflow, your SaaS site can bloom into an immersive experience that captivates and converts, and you won't have to wait for a developer to bring your vision to reality. It’s empowering, it’s effective, and it might just be the no-code solution you’ve been searching for.

Carrd

Carrd shines as the beacon of simplicity in the no-code universe, offering an intuitive platform for those wanting to make a splash without the deep dive into complex website development. 

It's the digital equivalent of a Swiss watch – minimalist, precise, and incredibly efficient.

At its core, Carrd specialises in one-page websites, and it does so with elegance. It strips away the clutter and complexity that can often overwhelm the website creation. 

With Carrd, you start with a blank canvas, and within minutes, you can have a single-page site that's not only functional but also polished and professional. 

It’s perfect for those initial forays into the online world where a multi-page site is overkill, and a one-page site can say it all.

Carrd's user interface is a study in a user-friendly design. There's no jargon, no complex menus, just a straightforward drag-and-drop builder that guides you through the process with visual cues and a clear pathway to completion. This makes it an ideal choice for entrepreneurs, freelancers, and creatives who need an online presence fast, without the need to learn web development or design.

But don't let the simplicity fool you. Despite its straightforward approach, Carrd is robust enough to create a landing page that's sleek, responsive, and tailored to your needs. 

Want to showcase your SaaS product? Carrd can help you put together a page that highlights your features, collects sign-ups, and even integrates with other platforms for email campaigns or user analytics.

The beauty of Carrd lies in its agility. Need to launch a marketing campaign or validate a business idea with a landing page? 

Carrd’s streamlined process means you can go from concept to live site in the time it takes to have a coffee. It's about immediacy and impact, providing a no-fuss approach that respects your time and your audience's attention span.

Furthermore, Carrd comes with various design templates and customisation options, giving you the freedom to infuse your brand's personality into your site. 

From fonts and colours to layout and imagery, you can tweak and tune every element to reflect your SaaS product's essence.

In the digital age where attention is the new currency, Carrd helps you make every pixel count. It empowers you to create a focused, high-impact landing page that captures and converts. 

For the SaaS builder looking for rapid deployment and an impressive digital footprint without getting bogged down by complexity, Carrd is the quintessential one-page wonder.

Bubble: The SaaS Incubator

Bubble is a bit like the Swiss Army knife for SaaS builders who are serious about crafting complex applications but would rather not get tangled in the web of coding. 

It's the kind of platform that's geared up for the big leagues — designed to handle the demands of building sophisticated, multi-user SaaS applications that can run smoothly on both desktop and mobile web browsers.

Let's break it down: Bubble's interactive interface is a dream for creators. It’s where functionality meets creativity, giving you the freedom to design not just the user interface but also the logic that powers the application. 

With Bubble, you've got a smorgasbord of tools and elements at your fingertips, allowing you to integrate databases, design workflows, and manage your app’s entire lifecycle from one central hub. 

It's like being the conductor of an orchestra, with every instrument and note playing exactly as you command, without needing to know how to play each one yourself.

The true beauty of Bubble lies in its ability to scale. In the digital world, growth can happen fast — sometimes overnight. 

You need infrastructure that can keep up with that pace without buckling under pressure. That's what Bubble brings to the table. 

It's robust yet flexible, able to support your SaaS as it grows from a handful of users to thousands, without forcing you to pause and retool the underlying architecture. 

It's almost like having an elastic band that stretches with your needs, ensuring that your foundation is solid yet not rigid.

Bubble goes beyond just app creation; it's about creating an environment where your SaaS can thrive. 

The platform's capabilities extend to performance monitoring, version control, and even hosting, which means you can focus on the product and the users rather than the operational nitty-gritty. Bubble doesn’t just give you the tools to build; it gives you the space to grow.

Scaling with Bubble isn't just a technical process; it's a strategic one. 

The platform’s capacity to handle complex, data-driven applications means you can start simple and then add layers of sophistication as your user base expands and your business model evolves. 

With Bubble, the usual growing pains of scaling a SaaS application are turned into growth spurts, propelling your business forward with confidence and agility.

Zoho Creator: The Process Master

Zoho Creator is the ace up the sleeve for businesses looking to streamline and automate their processes with a cloud-based application. 

It's not just another no-code platform; it's a solution with a laser-sharp focus on transforming business operations. 

If your aim is to create an app that's not only efficient but also smart and connected, Zoho Creator is a tool worth considering.

With Zoho Creator, the complexities of app development dissolve into a simple drag-and-drop affair. 

Here's where it gets interesting: you’re not just moving around shapes and texts; you’re constructing workflows and processes. 

This platform is all about enabling you to create applications that work for you — think of it as hiring an expert team of developers, and then streamlining their expertise into an intuitive interface that you control with a click and a drag.

Whether it's pulling data from your CRM, syncing with your accounting software, or connecting with your customer support platform, Zoho Creator makes it look easy. 

The result? A well-oiled machine where data flows seamlessly from one system to another, breaking down silos and creating a unified ecosystem for your SaaS operation.

Zoho Creator is endowed with the capability to automate workflows, which means repetitive tasks can be set on autopilot, freeing up valuable time and resources. 

Whether it's sending out invoices, updating records, or generating reports, automation turns these time-consuming tasks into background operations that run with precision and reliability.

To cap it off, Zoho Creator is built with the understanding that businesses evolve. It's flexible enough to grow with your operation, adding new functionalities and scaling up as your SaaS company expands. 

This isn't just about getting an app up and running; it's about laying a foundation for growth that can adapt and evolve over time.

In the world of no-code SaaS builders, Zoho Creator is not just a tool; it's a business partner that works tirelessly behind the scenes, ensuring your operation runs smoothly, efficiently, and intelligently. 

With Zoho Creator at your side, you're not just building apps; you're crafting a digital ecosystem tailored to your business needs.

Thunkable: The Mobile Mogul

In the arena of no-code development, Thunkable stands tall, especially when it comes to building mobile applications for iOS and Android. 

This platform is for the innovators who want to translate their SaaS ideas into mobile experiences. 

Thunkable's user interface is akin to playing with Lego — it's hands-on, intuitive, and it simplifies the complex coding that typically goes into mobile app development.

The drag-and-drop canvas and the logical blocks are the building blocks of Thunkable, allowing even those with zero coding expertise to assemble a robust mobile app piece by piece. 

It's about giving life to your app with touch-friendly interfaces, smooth transitions, and an overall native feel that mobile users expect. 

And when it comes to integration, Thunkable plays well with others. Whether you need to connect to APIs, databases, or other services, it ensures that your app is not an island but a connected part of the larger digital ecosystem.

Appy Pie: The Automation Wizard

Appy Pie emerges as a champion in the no-code field, blending the creation of web and mobile applications with powerful automation capabilities. 

It's more than just an app builder; it's a wizard that automates workflows, creates chatbots, and integrates with a multitude of web services, all within an all-in-one platform. 

The simplicity of Appy Pie is alluring — it promises and delivers a way to create complex applications as easily as pie, without ever needing to preheat an oven.

Beyond building applications, Appy Pie allows you to automate mundane tasks, integrate with existing tools, and even bring AI-driven chatbots into your customer service arsenal. 

It's designed for those who want to streamline their operations and provide a seamless user experience without the need to code or hire a developer.

Bravo Studio: The Figma-to-App Alchemist

Bravo Studio serves as a bridge between design and functionality, specifically for those who use Figma or Adobe XD. 

It's where your beautifully designed interfaces are not just static images but are transformed into fully functioning applications for both iOS and Android. 

Bravo Studio is akin to an alchemist's lab where the design gold is transmuted into the apps of your dreams.

For designers who have invested time in crafting the perfect UI/UX in Figma or Adobe XD, Bravo Studio is the catalyst that makes these designs interactive without losing any of the design fidelity. 

It's a game-changer for design-led SaaS businesses that want to ensure their mobile presence reflects their aesthetic and functional vision.

ManyChat: The Conversationalist

ManyChat stands out in the digital landscape as a conversational architect, allowing you to create chatbots for various platforms with unprecedented ease. 

In an era where instant communication is king, ManyChat gives you the tools to build bots that are not just functional but also personable, without writing a single line of code.

Whether it's for Facebook Messenger, Instagram, email, or SMS, ManyChat's intuitive drag-and-drop editor and diverse templates provide a seamless bot-building experience. 

It's about enhancing customer interaction, ensuring that your SaaS can communicate effectively with its users, anytime and anywhere.

Stage: The Collaborative Creator

Stage is the digital canvas where collaboration meets creation. It's designed for teams to come together to prototype and build applications with a focus on enhancing the user experience. 

Stage's visual interface, combined with a rich library of components and widgets, facilitates a cooperative environment where ideas can be shared, tested, and refined in real-time.

This platform is especially suited for SaaS teams that rely on agility and feedback to iterate quickly. 

It encourages a dynamic design process where every team member can contribute to the application's development, ensuring that the end product is well-rounded and user-centric.

GameSalad: The Game Changer

For SaaS ventures that want to gamify their services or create standalone gaming experiences, GameSalad presents itself as the no-code solution. 

It's where gaming meets development without the complexity that usually comes with game programming. 

With GameSalad, you’re not just building games — you're crafting interactive experiences that can captivate and engage users.

GameSalad's visual programming interface is designed for game designers and educators who want to bring their visions to life on web, Android, and iOS platforms. 

It offers a suite of tools that make game development accessible, whether you're looking to create simple puzzles or more complex adventures. 

GameSalad is the platform for those who see their SaaS product not just as a service but as an experience that can be as engaging and entertaining as a game.

Conclusion 

In the bustling landscape of SaaS development, the rise of no-code platforms has been a game-changer, democratizing the ability to bring digital products to life. 

From the visual website creations of Webflow to the streamlined one-page wonders crafted with Carrd, the no-code movement is empowering creators at every level. 

Whether you're a seasoned entrepreneur or a newcomer to the digital space, the power to create is now more accessible than ever.

As we've explored these diverse no-code builders, one thing is clear: the future of SaaS development is bright and boundless. 

With such powerful platforms at your fingertips, the only limit is your imagination. 

So go ahead, choose your champion among these titans of no-code and start building the future of SaaS — one block, one bot, one app at a time.

Need an expert’s outlook? We’ll be happy to help.

Title: B2B SaaS Website Best Practices: Everything You Should Know in 2023
Author: team-epyc
Content:
If we ask you point blank, what is the one thing that makes a website stand out of the rest?

Well, most of you would say “the design”, some would go with “content”, while the rest might find aspects like speed and positioning of the website as non-negotiable.

In 2022, the global SaaS market reached over $261.15 billion.

With a growth expectation of 214% by the end of this decade.

Taking the market evaluation to a staggering $819.23 billion.  👀


But amid all these fancy growth charts, lie founders, marketers and designers like yourself who are hustling round the clock to figure the best possible outcome for their SaaS websites.

At EPYC, we love it when we come across passionate SaaS founders looking to build a solid website for their services.

Over the years we have helped platforms like NovaBenefits, Docusumo and Nektar.ai turn heads through their compelling websites.

With so much back and forth in the SaaS space, specially with websites; how they should be, how they should look like or function.

We thought, why not share our piece of expertise with all you hustlers out there.

Hence, if you are a founder, a marketer, a designer or someone just curious about how a compelling SaaS website is put together. This post will assist you in all aspects!

1. We have to say this; Choose No-Code!

When you are creating a website for your SaaS platform it is for one sole purpose; to act as your marketing front that is quick. 🥷

Being a no-code development agency ourselves, we run across several SaaS founders entangled in the complexities of building hi-tech websites traditionally.

Our advice? Well, just close your eyes and choose no-code!

In comparison to traditional websites, platforms like Webflow or WordPress can be lifesavers when you are trying to build an effective SaaS website, why?

Both the platforms offer:

  1. User-friendly interfaces.

  2. Speedy web development

  3. Flexibility for showcasing unique features.

They are SEO-friendly (a big thumbs up), ensuring improved online visibility, and offer responsive designs for optimal mobile experiences.

Incremental changes are faster with these platforms, making them scalable, therefore, allowing you to grow and adapt your websites over time “with ease”.

In case of any challenge or anomaly, their active user communities provide access to support and ample resources.

And, if you are a startup or a smaller SaaS firm, each of these platforms would fit right within your budget. 💰

Plus, their focus on security safeguards user data, while their robust content management capabilities facilitate easy content creation and updates, making them ideal choices.

Ideally a SaaS website should be handled by the marketers or GTM members. Which is only possible if you opt for a solution friendly to the non-tech

2. Position your product right

Positioning is all about carving out a special spot for your brand or product in the minds of your target audience. The goal?

To shape how people see you, so you can get a leg up on the competition.

When you nail your positioning strategy, you're not just making people aware of your brand—you're convincing them to actually buy from you.

The tricky part is that positioning can be pretty subtle, so you often notice it best when you look at it from a customer's point of view.

Remember this folks: To nail the positioning of your SaaS website you need to understand the concept of Association and Dissociation!

What do we mean by this?

Look at how Webflow has tried to associate with its user base over time:

The first stage

The second iteration

Vs. what we see now

They evolved to “Build with the power of code - without writing any” (A beautifully positioned product, that associates with the end users intent “which is to build a website” and disassociate from rest by adding “ without writing code”)

Some of our favourite products who are acing the positioning game;

Toptal.com

“Hire the Top 3% of Freelance Talent” - The first thing you see on their website, you don’t even need a scroll to understand what Toptal is about.

That’s how you out-position your competitors. 🧠

Beehiiv.com

“The Newsletter Platform Built For Growth” - A message that satisfies the user’s query and intent.

Beehiive’s positioning is definitely out of the box.

The best way to make your user understand your offerings in a glimpse is to answer these questions:

Who Is the Target Audience?

Understanding who you're selling to helps tailor your message and choose the right channels.

What Makes the Product Unique?

Identify the key features or benefits that set your product apart from competitors.

What Problem Does It Solve?

 Knowing the problem you're solving can help you focus your messaging and connect with customers.

How Do You Want Customers to Feel?

Emotions drive purchases. Decide what feelings you want to evoke through your product positioning.

Where Does It Fit in the Market?

Understanding your market position helps you decide pricing, promotion strategies, and how to differentiate your product.

One of the non-negotiables while positioning a product is to categorize it right, Here’s how we would position these global products if it were to us:

1. Notion as "Evernote for Teams"

Notion offers note-taking, but with enhanced collaboration features, making it ideal for team settings.

2. Webflow as "WordPress for Designers"

Webflow offers the customization of WordPress but adds a layer of design flexibility that appeals to graphic designers.

3. Asana as "Slack for Project Management"

Asana takes the real-time communication benefits of Slack and applies them to project management.‍

4.Canva as "Adobe Photoshop for Non-Designers"

Canva provides design tools that are user-friendly for those who aren't trained in graphic design.

5. Trello as "Excel for Visual People"

Trello takes the organizational functionality of Excel and turns it into a more visual, drag-and-drop experience.

3. Let’s talk about “The Design” 🎨

As we learn more about the intricacies involved in design, it becomes more crucial to build a website that talks to your customers visually.

The smallest of design elements can make enormous difference, here’s an intriguing read on the significance of “a line”, literally a line and you’ll be amazed at how these small elements can reshape your website's experience.

Well if we dig deeper, It's not just about making things look pretty, although that's important too.

It's about crafting an intuitive and user-friendly interface (UI) that,

  1. Fosters positive user experiences (UX).

  2. Engages with the end user

  3. Offers seamless onboarding.

Think of it like this: a well-designed site is like a well-organized shop. It's easy to find what you need, and feels trustworthy.

But here's the real magic: it will turn using your software into a habit. Just like we reach for our favorite apps without even thinking, you want your SaaS website to be that go-to tool for your users.

It's about creating an experience that not only works well but feels like second nature.

4. What’s the fuss about Micro Writing? 🤔

If you want your end user to seamlessly navigate through your website, you have to make sure every piece of content works as signboards.

One slight mis-direction can lose you a bunch of potential customers.

Here’s an example how a slight change in the microcopy helped Google with a whopping 17% increased engagement in their hospitality searches.

Microcopy is like the friendly expert who's always there to assist your customer. It's those tiny snippets of text that pop up exactly when you need them.

  • Want to explain a feature?

  • Asking to sign up or fill out a form?

The microcopy should explain it in plain language (not vanilla), provide clear instructions, ensure potential clients don't get lost in the process, and make the most of the software's capabilities.

It adds a unique personality to the website, connecting the customer  and your brand.

When something goes wrong or there’s an error, the microcopy should step in to reassure and guide your customers on how to resolve the issue, preventing frustration, and resulting in decreased bounce-back rates.

5. Add social proof, some more social proof and yes, some more

Okay, this might sound a little too much, but trust us when we say there’s nothing like “not needed” social proof.

Everything counts,

  1. Case studies,        

  2. Success metrics,

  3. Customer Logos,

  4. Client testimonials,

  5. Third party reviews, or,

  6. Transparency around ROI.


Your customer needs validation about a crucial decision they are going to make; ensure you give them enough reason.

You can enhance the whole experience by adding certain elements like video testimonials or get your platform recommended by industry experts through podcasts, social media posts, or blogs.

In a world where 93% of online shoppers read reviews before purchasing, adding extra third-party reviews will only benefit.

Nonetheless, integrating in-depth case studies and success metrics will help you engage with your audience better and will create more credibility for your SaaS platform.

And, make sure your website has most of these social proofs out there, follow us again “out there”.

We want to emphasize this, because we often find these gems hidden deep inside the website, sometimes impossible to navigate.

Authors tip: If you are selling in the USA then reviews from G2 and Trust Pilot matters.

6. Your SaaS website should be quick, PERIOD!⚡️

There’s no denying the fact that a slow website can get you in trouble.

Studies have found that websites loading within 2 seconds enjoy a mere 9% bounce rate, while a five-second wait increases it to a significant 38%.

Even a 1 second delay in page response can result in a 7% dip in conversions. Whereas, 40% of users won't stick around for more than three seconds.

Mobile users are especially sensitive to speed, with 73% encountering frustratingly slow sites.‍

To back this data, we’ve curated some of the fastest loading SaaS websites and you’ll not be shocked to find some of your favourites and industry leaders here.

  • Salesforce (461 milliseconds)

  • Google Workspace (1.88 seconds)

  • HubSpot (1.08 seconds)

  • Dropbox (1.73 seconds)

  • Shopify (1.78 seconds)

These numbers speak volumes about the relentless pursuit of speed. The lesson is crystal clear: in today's digital landscape, where every millisecond counts, speedy load times are non-negotiable.

So, get rid of speed hoggers like unnecessary animations or heavy graphics that add no significant value.

A quick website is the bedrock of user satisfaction and can significantly impact the success and user engagement of your SaaS platform.

Remember, a good SaaS website should display all the social proofs clearly.

Nothing like if you can somehow adjust it on your home page.

7. Mobile-first Responsive Design

In a world where 54% of all the web traffic comes from mobile devices, optimizing your SaaS website for the same becomes crucial.

But you might think, most of your target audience would navigate your or any SaaS platform via their laptops or desktops right?

It’s just not a thing you would search on your mobile.

The catch here is, what about all the traffic that is generated through backlinks which you would’ve promoted on LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook or any other platform.

As most of these users use these platforms on their mobile devices, it’s natural that they click on those links through the same.

Follow these practices to make sure your website works seamlessly on mobile devices.

1. Focus on Essential Content

In a mobile-first approach, content takes center stage. Given the limited screen space on mobile devices, it's crucial for you to highlight the most important elements.

We are talking about the pieces of content that users will most likely be searching for.

2. Simplify User Navigation

Creating a smooth navigation experience is vital for mobile users.

You can make use of features like navigation drawers or Hamburger menus to showcase secondary website elements.

This ensures that users can effortlessly locate the information they need.

3. Minimise Intrusive Pop-ups

Due to the limited screen space on mobile devices, pop-ups and ads can be particularly annoying for users.

It's essential to keep the user's needs focused and avoid any elements that could disrupt their browsing experience.

4. Conduct Real-World Device Testing

The best way to confirm that your website performs well across various devices is to test it under real-world conditions.

This allows designers to check how the site appears and functions on different devices, including mobiles, tablets, and desktops, ensuring a consistent user experience.

Lastly, make sure you optimize the speed of your website for mobile devices specifically.

As we discussed earlier, slower websites can cause significant bounce-back rates.

Some final words of advice

  1. Don't get caught up in all the glitter and glam; focus on the gold, which means emphasising on what your audience cares about.

  1. Don't try to reinvent the wheel. See what's working for successful folks in your industry and learn from their playbook.

  1. SaaS websites don't have to be boring. Inject some excitement, make your site interesting, like the life of the party!

  1. Videos are your secret weapon; use them to explain stuff or show off your product's superpowers.

  1. Lastly, DON’T HIDE THOSE DEMOS. Make them easy to navigate and book.

Ending Note

Your website is not just a digital business card; it's your 24/7 sales representative.

It’s undeniably important to understand what goes behind shaping a state-of-the-art SaaS website, that not only:

  1. Looks dope,

  2. Or reflects your brand's identity,

  3. But also engages and converts visitors into valuable clients.

But don't stop here!

To truly elevate and upgrade your SaaS website game, it's essential to stay up-to-date with the latest trends and technologies.

We’ve built 70+ websites at EPYC and delivered great results.

3 things to always keep in mind to while building a great SaaS website:

  • Regularly revisit your site,

  • Gather user feedback, and,

  • Make improvements accordingly.
    ‍
    If you still feel some gaps to fill or your requirements are a little more intricate,  click here to schedule a free consultation with our experts.

    We’ll be happy to assist you in your endeavours! 🏆

Title: Picking The Right No-Code Backend Platform: A Buyer’s Guide in 2023
Author: team-epyc
Content:
If you are an SME relying on a limited budget, achieving a resilient backend might just be an ambiguous approach, thanks to the extensive cost and expertise required in creating and handling the project.

Over the past, we have spoken highly of how no-code tools have revolutionized the way we look towards web/app development.

Leading the charge are platforms like Webflow, Bubble, and Glide, among others have played a vital role in solidifying no-code adoption across various industries.

However, while most of these platforms excel in facilitating front-end development, the real engine driving your application's functionality lies in the backend.

In this guide, we will explore how no-code backend solutions are creating a shift in the market, particularly among MSMEs operating on a smaller budget.

Additionally, we will pour our expertise into helping you choose the right no-code backend platform for your tailored requirements.

What is No-Code Backend?


A no-code backend simplifies the backend development process by eliminating the need for manual code writing.

It allows you to construct the backend elements of your application like:

  • Relational databases,

  • Data storage,

  • Permissions,

  • Requests, and

  • API endpoints

using visual development tools, These tools chiefly comprise:

  • Pre-arranged development building blocks.

  • Ready-made app templates.

  • A visual interface.

  • A drag & drop system,

enabling you to select a template, tweak it with the given building blocks, and put together your web or mobile apps without the manual code-writing chore.

Here are some advantages of choosing a no-code backend:

  • Craft business logic and automate workflows with zero coding requirements.

  • Get your backend up and running in mere minutes sans the hassle of server management.

  • Effortlessly scale your app to cater to millions of users without getting bogged down in technicalities.

  • Enjoy the flexibility of connecting your app to any front-end, be it no-code or traditional coding platforms.

  • Design no-code APIs with ease, capable of transforming, filtering, and integrating data from diverse sources.

Key considerations while choosing the right no-code backend tool

1. Ease of Use:

Drag-and-Drop Interface: A hallmark of a user-friendly no-code platform is its drag-and-drop interface. This feature allows you to visually create and modify your website, reducing the dependency on technical expertise.

Learning Curve: Every platform has a learning curve, but the goal is to find one that's gentle enough for your team. A platform with intuitive design and a wealth of educational resources can accelerate the onboarding process.

2. Scalability:

Resource Allocation: As your business burgeons, so does the demand on your website. It's crucial that the platform can dynamically allocate resources to handle increased data, traffic, and functional requirements.

Upgrades: Explore the upgrade paths the platform offers. Seamless upgrades with minimal downtime are ideal for ensuring that your website stays current with the evolving tech landscape.

3. 3rd Party Integration:

APIs and Plugins: The ability to integrate with other services enriches your website’s functionality. Look for platforms that support a plethora of APIs and plugins, ensuring smooth inter-operation with third-party services.

Data Import/Export: A platform that simplifies data import and export will save you time and ensure that your operations run smoothly, especially during migrations or integrations.

4. Security:

Hosting Location: The geographical location where your data is hosted has legal and regulatory implications. Understand the laws governing data security and privacy in the hosting region.

Data Encryption: Robust encryption practices are fundamental to safeguarding sensitive data from unauthorized access.

Compliance: Ensure that the platform adheres to industry-specific compliance standards like GDPR, HIPAA, or PCI DSS.

5. Cost:

Pricing Model: Delve into the pricing model of the platform. Whether it’s a one-time fee, subscription-based, or a pay-as-you-go model, understanding the cost implications is crucial.

Hidden Costs: Be on the lookout for any concealed costs. Extra charges for additional features, data storage, or higher tiers of service can quickly add up.

6. Community and Support:

Documentation: Comprehensive, clear documentation is a treasure trove of information for onboarding, troubleshooting, and mastering the platform.

Customer Support: Exceptional customer support, ready to assist when issues arise, is invaluable. Look for platforms with a strong support infrastructure including live chat, email, and phone support.

Community: A vibrant, active community can be a source of innovative solutions, advice, and camaraderie as you navigate your digital journey.

7. SEO Capabilities:

URL Structure: An SEO-friendly URL structure facilitates better indexing by search engines, thereby improving your site's visibility.

Meta Tags: Being able to easily manage meta tags is indispensable for SEO. This includes editing title tags, meta descriptions, and other on-page SEO elements.

8. Analytics:

Built-in Analytics: Built-in analytics tools enable you to monitor and analyze your website’s performance and user engagement.

Third-party Tools: Integration with third-party analytics tools like Google Analytics provides deeper insights and a broader data analysis scope.

9. Reviews and Testimonials:

User Reviews: Sift through user reviews to glean insights on the platform’s strengths and weaknesses from real-world users.

Case Studies: Case studies offer a window into how the platform fares in practical, real-world scenarios, and how other businesses have leveraged it to meet their goals.

5 of our favourite no-code backend tools

1. Back4app:

Overview: Back4App is a platform that allows for easy creation and management of applications, with features such as real-time queries, a schema builder, and automated backups.

It supports GraphQL and REST APIs, making it a flexible choice for various development needs​.

Pricing:

  • Basic: $4.99 per user per month​​.

  • Pay as You Go: $80 per app per month, billed annually​.

  • Free/From: $25 per month​.

2. Xano:

Overview: Xano is a backend-as-a-service platform that simplifies backend development.

It offers a range of tools for database management, API generation, and third-party integrations, catering to both developers and non-technical users​.

Pricing:

  • Build: Free, with certain limitations​.

  • Scale 1x: $199 per month, billed annually​.

3. Backendless:

Overview: A visual app development platform allowing for mobile and web application creation without coding. Features include real-time capabilities, user management, and a codeless logic builder​.

Pricing:

  • Basic: $25 per month.

  • Free plan available with limited features​​

4. Airtable:

Overview: Primarily a powerful online database tool with a spreadsheet-like interface, it supports data management, real-time collaboration, and has API capabilities for integrations​.

Pricing:

  • Plus: $12 per user per month​​.

  • Pro: $24 per user per month​​.

  • There's also a $10 per seat per month plan​.

5. Supabase:

Overview: Supabase is an open-source alternative to Firebase, offering a backend-as-a-service with database, authentication, and storage solutions. It is known for its real-time PostgreSQL database, auto-generated APIs, and user authentication management.

Pricing:

  • $25 per month per organization or per project, with provisions like daily backups, 8GB database space, 100GB file storage, and 250GB bandwidth included​.

Concluding Thoughts

Navigating the digital realm requires a robust backend, and no-code platforms are bridging the gap, especially for SMEs and MSMEs on a budget.

Platforms like:

  • Back4app

  • Xano

  • Backendless

  • Airtable, and

  • Supabase
    are at the forefront, offering unique features at various price points to cater to different needs.

These platforms democratize app development, providing a user-friendly, cost-effective, and scalable solution.

The key considerations outlined in this guide aim to assist you in making an informed decision based on your project's requirements and your team’s expertise.

The no-code movement is not just a trend but a significant shift towards making app development accessible to all.

As you delve into selecting a no-code backend platform, focus on your project's long-term goals, ensuring the platform you choose is a perfect fit for your digital journey.

Need an expert outlook on your project?

Feel free to contact us, we’ll be happy to assist you in your no-code journey.

Title: Can You Really Build Complex Web Apps Using WebFlow in 2023?
Author: team-epyc
Content:
We all love Webflow; it stands out from the rest when it comes to creating beautiful yet functional marketing or e-commerce websites.

But, if you are a long-time user, you might already know how painful it is to build advanced web apps on Webflow. *It’s just not meant for it right?*

Although, numerous complex websites are built on Webflow, still building a compelling Web app can be challenging on the platform

So, if your project demands a complex web application and until now you thought Webflow was incapable of achieving so, this blog will add some perspective to your research. 🧑‍💻

Engineering advanced Webflow Web apps

When it comes to creating web applications with Webflow, the picture isn't entirely clear-cut.

As it stands, Webflow won't let you craft a web app that matches the sophistication of something like Asana, Doornash, Notion, or similar. You might achieve some similarities, but the end result will probably lack smoothness.

However, that's not to say you can't build anything. User-related data, such as Date of Birth, Age, or Location, can easily be linked to a user account and displayed through collection items.

Moreover, the ability to incorporate additional data sets is gradually improving in Webflow, thanks to certain tools, workarounds and the addition of this super cool no-code backend tool Wized.

This means that constructing specific dashboards is becoming increasingly straightforward, especially with the addition of integrations.

Webflow x Wized, made in heaven

Wized is this fantastic cloud-based no-code backend tool designed specifically for Webflow that makes building websites and web apps a breeze.

Here's the deal: Wized is not just your ordinary Webflow integration. It's like the dynamic duo of web development.

Instead of using complicated CMS systems like some other apps, Wized basically leverages Webflow's robust UI framework and does its magic on the backend to offer an intuitive and feature-rich platform for designing web applications..

It:

  1. Manages Data

  2. Lets you build a payment flow with Stripe.

  3. Lets you add logic to your web app.

  4. Handles authentication.

  5. Lets you make API calls easily.

It's all about simplicity and effectiveness.

Picture this: You can effortlessly create login/signup functionalities, integrate payment systems, and smoothly import/export data, all while making your web applications look stunning.

Users can easily create and customize UI elements using Webflow's drag-and-drop features, while also accessing its extensive library of pre-built components and templates.

Built on Webflow's responsive design capabilities, Wized ensures that user interfaces adapt seamlessly across various screen sizes, offering a consistent and optimized experience on desktop, tablet, and mobile.

You can build a variety of advanced web apps using Wized in conjunction with Webflow.

Here are the types of web apps you can create using Wized:

  1. Membership Apps: Create exclusive content and features for registered users.

  1. Marketplaces: Build platforms where buyers and sellers can interact.

  1. Content Gating: Restrict access to certain parts of your website until users meet specific criteria.

  1. Apps with multiple user authentication: Add secure login and registration features.

  1. Micro-SaaS Products: Launch small-scale software as a service application.

If we talk about websites built using Wized, you can look out for finsweet.com.  

Wized works seamlessly on top of Webflow, allowing you to integrate these functionalities without the need for additional coding or external tools.

Webflow X Memberstack

Memberstack simplifies the complexities that come with building membership-based websites.

It takes Webflow's already powerful design capabilities and supercharges them with features that make it easy to create a fully functional, member-focused platform.

Here's what Memberstack brings to the table:

  • User Authentication: Easily set up secure login and sign-up functionalities.

  • Payment Integration: Seamlessly integrate Stripe to manage subscriptions and one-time payments.

  • Content Gating: Control who sees what by setting up member-only access to specific content.

  • Custom Forms and Fields: Collect additional information from your members effortlessly.

  • Member Profiles: Allow users to have their own profiles, complete with customizable fields.

  • API Access: Integrate with other tools and platforms through a robust API.

Imagine this: You're building a content-rich Webflow web app. With Memberstack, you can effortlessly set up a paywall, allowing only subscribed members to access premium content.

You can also create a community forum, a private dashboard, or even a full-fledged e-commerce platform with member-only perks.

Here are some web apps built using Webflow and Memberstack:

Jetboost: A dynamic search and filter tool specifically designed for Webflow websites. It uses Memberstack for user authentication and membership features.

Makerpad: An educational platform that offers tutorials on no-code tools. It uses Webflow for its front-end and Memberstack for managing its community and paid memberships.

Swipe Pages: A landing page builder that uses Webflow for its website and Memberstack for its user management and subscription services.

Nocode HQ: A marketplace for no-code templates and tutorials. It uses Webflow for its website design and Memberstack for user accounts and premium content access.

In a nutshell, Memberstack amplifies what you can achieve with Webflow, allowing you to build not just websites but comprehensive web platforms. It's all about making your life easier while delivering a top-notch user experience.

Webflow X Zapier

Zapier isn't just a simple add-on to Webflow; it's the catalyst that supercharges your Webflow projects, making them smarter and more automated.

So, what makes Zapier so special when used with Webflow?

It's all about,

  • Automation, and,

  • Connectivity.

Zapier takes Webflow's already impressive design and development capabilities and enhances them with automated workflows that connect your Webflow site to over 3,000 other apps.

It facilitates:

  • Automated Workflows: Set up "Zaps" to automate repetitive tasks between Webflow and other apps.

  • Data Syncing: Automatically sync data between your Webflow site and other platforms like Google Sheets, Mailchimp, or CRM systems.

  • Event Triggers: Use Webflow events like form submissions or new CMS items to trigger actions in other apps.

  • Conditional Logic: Implement if-this-then-that logic to create more complex automation sequences.

  • Multi-Step Zaps: Create workflows that involve multiple steps and conditions for more advanced automation.

  • API Integration: Connect to custom APIs to further extend your Webflow site's capabilities.

Picture this: You have an e-commerce site built on Webflow;

With Zapier, you can automate the entire customer journey, from the moment a user signs up, 👇


  • Their information can be sent to your CRM,

  • A welcome email can be triggered, and,

  • Any purchase activity can be logged automatically in your accounting software.

Types of web apps you can build using Webflow and Zapier:

  • Content Platforms: Automatically share new Webflow CMS items to social media or email newsletters.

  • E-commerce Stores: Sync inventory and automate order processing.

  • Customer Portals: Automate ticket creation for customer support queries.

  • Event Platforms: Send automated reminders or follow-ups for event registrations.

Here are some inspiring web apps built on Webflow

Scoop

Imagine having a handy tool that simplifies managing your team and their tasks.

With Scoop, you can effortlessly keep track of their time, skills, and whereabouts, and even stay in touch, all from one convenient place.

This user-friendly software allows your team to sync up on schedules and monitor productivity, while also offering a flexible desk setup that adapts to your employees' needs.

It's like having a helpful teammate to keep everything in check!"

Flow

Flow is like a digital swap meet where you can effortlessly trade, buy, or sell all sorts of digital stuff.

Whether you're into NFTs or other digital treasures, Flow's got your back.

What's neat about the platform is that it runs on blockchain tech, making it super secure and open for everyone.

They're even planning an upcoming event called an 'initial coin offering' where you can snag some FLO tokens. These tokens are like the currency of the Flow world, perfect for trading and having a blast on the platform.

Snappr

Snappr is your go-to buddy for capturing memorable moments! It’s not your average photo booth rental service. Snappr hooks you up with pro photographers at the snap of your fingers.

The platform? Oh-so-easy! Whether it's a birthday bash, a family reunion, or a fancy dinner, Snappr's got the perfect photographer for you.

And guess what? It's a breeze to book – just 90 seconds! All you gotta do is show up and strike a pose. Your memories, their magic!"

Ending Note

So, can you really build complex web apps using WebFlow in 2023?

The answer is a resounding yes, but with a few warnings. As long as you understand the platform's limitations and strengths, WebFlow can be an incredibly powerful tool in your web development arsenal.

If you're looking to streamline your development process without sacrificing quality, WebFlow is certainly worth considering.

Title: 5 Kickass SaaS Websites Built On Webflow
Author: team-epyc
Content:
