Now, when we talk about SaaS, you will most likely imagine a boring website filled with text and whatnot. Well, that's not the case anymore!

SaaS websites have evolved, and they have evolved big time.

These platforms are not just about presenting information; they're about delivering seamless user experiences, ensuring intuitive navigation, and integrating powerful functionalities—all while looking aesthetically pleasing.

Parallely, the intervention of platforms like Webflow has fueled this shift even further by opening the gates to the realm of website development for the masses.

As a Webflow development agency, we have designed some exciting SaaS websites. Moreover, we love when we come across more such UI/UX havens built on the platform .

Hence, we did the deed of curating some of the best SaaS websites built on Webflow “according to us”.

So, if you are trying to build your own SaaS website on Webflow and lurking for some design inspirations you are right where you need to be.

But before we hit the core. Let’s understand…

Why should you consider Webflow for your SaaS Website?

Webflow stands out as a website builder, significantly benefiting SaaS businesses due to its intuitive visual design interface. 

The drag-and-drop feature simplifies the design process, eliminating the dependency on developers for minor changes. 

Moreover, Webflow's robust and customizable CMS is ideal for SaaS companies requiring frequent updates to their product features or testimonials. 

The platform guarantees a responsive design, ensuring a uniform user experience across various devices. 

Its hosting infrastructure promises:

  • Fast load times, 

  • Enhanced user engagement and 

  • Reduced bounce rates 

Additionally, Webflow offers integrated SEO tools, aiding in improving your SaaS site's search engine rankings, and driving organic traffic.

5 Best SaaS websites built on Webflow

Design, services, and intractability of the website are the criterias we have kept in mind while curating this list. Let’s dive right in!

1. NovaBenefits

NovaBenefits isn't just another SaaS tool; it's a commitment to every employee's well-being. By offering personalized health benefits, the platform ensures that each individual receives the support tailored to their unique needs.

It is built around the challenges HRs and employers face, from juggling various tasks to getting bogged down by repetitive administrative chores. That's where Nova's tech-first approach shines.

Building NovaBenefits was nothing short of a lifetime experience for our team. We wanted the design or you can call it the outlook of the website to reflect the mission and vision of the company and the challenges it is trying to solve.

2. Docusumo

Building Docusumo has been one of the most interesting SaaS projects we have worked on. 

It is one of the world's most popular Document AI platforms that data-driven companies can only work with.

The idea with the home page was to make it elaborate yet minimal, we achieved this through moving texts and animations.

As the product aims to help businesses structure their documentation, we helped them design a website that resonates the same.

3. Loom

When it comes to showcasing the power of video in corporate communication, Loom stands out brilliantly. Built on Webflow, Loom's website is a testament to the proverb, "A picture is worth a thousand words." But in Loom's case, it's videos that steal the show.

At first glance, Loom's homepage is refreshingly light on text. But don't let that simplicity fool you. It's packed with value, offering visitors a dynamic experience through animations, engaging GIFs crafted from video-conferencing sessions, and sleek product mockups. The design approach is minimalistic, yet every element serves a purpose, guiding visitors towards the undeniable benefits of video communication.

The genius of Loom's website lies in its ability to convey the essence of its product without overwhelming users with jargon or excessive content. Instead, it captivates with visual storytelling, selling the idea of video as the future of corporate communications, one delightful visual at a time.

Loom undoubtedly earns a high spot in our list, not just for its design but for the innovative way it communicates its core value proposition.

4. Whimsical

Whimsical is a one-stop collaboration hub that seamlessly integrates essential tools for teams.

From intuitive flowcharts and design-centric wireframes to brainstorm-friendly mind maps and centralized docs, Whimsical is revolutionizing team collaboration.

As soon as you land on their site, you're greeted with a vibrant color splash, quirky background design elements, and glowing reviews from satisfied users.

If the homepage doesn't immediately captivate you, just navigate to one of their feature landing pages. There, you'll be treated to a visual feast of colorful illustrations, animations, and demo videos that vividly showcase the power of their tools. Whimsical's approach to visual communication is truly a game-changer.

5. Grow

Grow is redefining the way B2B SaaS startups appeal to the corporate world. At its core, the platform simplifies the process for companies to connect, explore, and derive insights from their data, ensuring data-driven decisions across the board.

Their no-code full-stack business intelligence (BI) platform is a testament to this, merging data integrations, warehousing, and visualization into one cohesive platform.

While many startups grapple with maintaining their unique identity, Grow strikes a perfect balance. Opting for a darker, muted color palette, they've sidestepped the typical SaaS jargon in favor of straightforward and descriptive slogans.

Targeting enterprise clients keen on advanced business analytics, Grow reinforces its credibility with prominent ratings, showcased clients, and genuine testimonials. The detailed screenshots further solidify Grow as a reliable partner for businesses aiming to harness the power of data.

Concluding Thoughts

Webflow has emerged as a powerful platform for creating dynamic and visually appealing websites, with many SaaS brands leveraging its capabilities to the fullest.

The 5 websites we discussed are prime examples of its potential. Our aim with this blog was to highlight some of the best SaaS websites in the industry and showcase the versatility of Webflow.

As the digital landscape continues to evolve, and SaaS platforms gain more traction. Technologies like Webflow will undoubtedly play a pivotal role in shaping the future of online design and user experience.

Want to build your SaaS website on Webflow? Feel free to reach us out, we’ll be happy to assist you.

Title: 6 Rookie Mistakes To Avoid When Using Webflow Custom Code
Author: team-epyc
Content:
Envision this; You've just spent weeks, maybe even months, designing your dream website on Webflow.

You've poured over every detail, from the pixel-perfect layout to the smooth animations. Your website looks fantastic, but you decide to kick things up a notch.

You think, "I’ll just add some custom code to make my site even more interactive and unique.

You write custom code for a cool new feature you found online. You hit publish and eagerly check your site.

But wait, what's this?

Instead of that slick new element, you're greeted by a jumbled mess of overlapping text and images. Or even worse, a feature that was working perfectly is now completely unresponsive.

If this scenario feels all too familiar, you're not alone. While Webflow's custom code capabilities can supercharge your site's functionality, they can also be a double-edged sword.

As a seasoned Webflow agency, we've catered to a diverse range of customer needs. Over time, we've encountered a set of recurring challenges.

Drawing from our vast experience, we've curated this guide to spotlight 6 typical missteps even the best Webflow users sometimes make when incorporating custom code.

Our aim? To let you learn from our experiences.

But first, let’s understand,

What is Webflow Custom Code and Why use it?

Webflow Custom Code allows you to add your own

  • HTML,

  • CSS, and

  • JavaScript

Directly into your Webflow project. Think of it as a magic wand that lets you go beyond Webflow's built-in features, giving you the freedom to implement unique functionalities and design elements.

Whether adding a specific animation, integrating third-party APIs, or enhancing user interaction, custom code gives you the power to make it happen.

Now, you might wonder, "Why should I bother with custom code when Webflow is all about deploying awesome websites without code?"!

While Webflow's built-in features are robust and work alongside the idea of no code, they do have their limits.

Here are some reasons why you might want to utilize custom code:

  • Unique Features: Stand out from the crowd by adding functionalities like Animation or a custom slider that aren't available through Webflow's default options

  • Custom Interactions: Maybe you want to engage your users in a specific way that Webflow’s pre-made interactions don't cover. Custom code has your back.

  • Performance/SEO Optimization: Webflow custom code allows you to make under-the-hood tweaks for better site performance, improving SEO rankings and user experience.

  • Third-Party Integrations: Whether it's a specialized tracking script, an external widget,or sending form data to CRMs like Hubspot. Webflow custom code provides a pathway for seamless third-party integrations.

  • Complete Control: When you use custom code, you gain complete control over your website's behavior, look, and feel, which can be empowering for those who know how to wield it.

What can go wrong while deploying Webflow Custom Code? 🆘

A lot of things can go wrong while deploying Webflow custom code, hence, here are 6 of the most common ones we can help you mitigate.

1. Managing Both Production and Development Versions of Your Code

It's essential to maintain separate versions of your code for development and production.

Mixing them up or accidentally publishing the development version can lead to:

  • Inconsistencies

  • Bugs

  • Sub-optimal user experience

An EPYC tip: To avoid such confusions make sure you use Github/Gitlab.

Hosting and deploying code from here instead of Webflow will ensure that you always revert back without the need of publishing the website.

2. The blind spot of Webflow’s Code Editor

The Webflow code editor doesn’t facilitate:

  • In-built error checker

  • Testing the code.

This means if you have syntax or functional errors in your code, they won't be flagged immediately, but only once you publish the website.

The downside?

These errors only rear their heads when visitors land on your site.

It’s a bit like stepping out with mismatched shoes and only realizing it when someone points it out!

3. The Preview Challenge

One of Webflow’s strengths – its visual designer, becomes a bit of a hiccup when using custom code.

You can't preview the effect of your code directly within the designer. Instead, you need to take the extra step of publishing your site to see the code in action.

This can slow down your development process and may even result in unintentional errors being made public.

4. Code Placement Matters

Placing your custom code in the wrong section can break your website's functionality or appearance.

Whether it's the header, footer, or within an HTML embed element, ensuring the correct placement is crucial. Think of it as fitting puzzle pieces - one wrong piece can ruin the whole picture.

An EPYC tip: If you are trying to add scripts for Analytics or Chatbots, keep in mind that it should be added in the right place in <head> or <body> depending on the tool and guidelines.

5. The Risks of Exposed API Keys

Many Webflow developers enrich their sites by fetching data from third-party platforms.

However, there's a catch: Webflow's Custom Code section is client-side.

This means API keys, which are meant to be confidential, can get exposed to the public.

It not only risks your data's security but can also lead to unwanted costs if someone misuses the keys.

An EPYC tip: If you want to mitigate this risk, make sure you deploy a custom backend server or mask your API keys.

For external API’s use tools like Wized, which will enhance the security of your API integrations, and facilitate a seamless sensitive data handling.

5. Class naming anomalies

Webflow developers often use capital letters and spaces in their class names.

However, Webflow automatically changes uppercase letters to lowercase and replaces spaces with hyphens (-) when it publishes the website.

So, for example 'Hero Section' or 'Team Card', on the live website, becomes 'hero-section' or 'team-card.'

For example, let's say we have a box with the class name 'Blue Box,' and we want to write custom CSS to make its background color blue.

Final Word

It's evident that experience is a treasury of wisdom. As experts in the Webflow domain, we've faced our fair share of challenges and triumphs.

By sharing these insights, we hope to mitigate the risks you might encounter while deploying Webflow custom code.

Remember, every coding challenge presents an opportunity for growth. By rigorous learning and practise, you can navigate these challenges more effectively, ensuring that your projects not only meet but exceed expectations.

Now, ready to elevate your Webflow projects? Dive into our extensive resources or get in touch with our experts today for personalized guidance

Let's build better, together!

Title: Why Do Startups Want Apps Built on Bubble as their MVPs
Author: team-epyc
Content:
Are you an aspiring entrepreneur?

Are you looking for an app builder to shape your groundbreaking idea?

But the cost involved stumbles you down?

You're not alone. The journey from a brilliant concept to a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) and eventually the final product can often feel like walking through a maze blindfolded.

That's where Bubble, the revolutionary no-code app builder, stands tall as the game-changing platform that's turning heads in the SME landscape.

Why? Because it facilitates MVP development faster, simpler, and at a slimmer budget. In today's post, we're going to unpack why an increasing number of startups are turning to Bubble to get their MVPs up and running.

So, if you're on the fence about how to develop your MVP or just curious about why every startup wants their apps built on Bubble, stick around. This one's for you!

What is Bubble?

Did you ever play with Lego as a kid?

You know, those colorful pieces that you could snap together to make anything from a spaceship to a castle?

Well, think of Bubble as the adult version of Lego, but for app development. Yep, it's that simple and fun.

Bubble is one of the front-runners in the no-code movement, offering a powerful,

  • Point-and-click web editor along with,

  • Cloud hosting, and

  • Extensive backend functionalities.

This enables you to build,

  • Powerful Web applications

  • Strategic Workflows

that are as simple as prototypes or as complex as marketplaces and SaaS products. The sky's the limit, really! 🪐

What's even more exciting, is the thriving community of builders and entrepreneurs rallying around Bubble.

We're talking millions of users who are not just building but also launching successful businesses.

Some have even entered elite accelerator programs like Y Combinator and raised a jaw-dropping $365M in venture funding.

So, Bubble isn't just a product—it's a revolution. It's a strong, united community that believes technology creation should be accessible to everyone.

And the best part?

It's incredibly relevant in today's fast-paced tech world, where time is of the essence, and the market waits for no one.

Whether you're a startup or a solo entrepreneur, Bubble offers you the chance to build MVPs without breaking the bank or burning the midnight oil.

What’s an MVP and Why Should You Build One?

Picture this: You have a brilliant idea for an app that you believe will revolutionize the industry. But ideas are a dime a dozen, right? How do you know yours will stick?

An MVP, or Minimum Viable Product, is the most basic version of your product that still provides essential features and value to users. It's essentially a working model of your idea stripped down to its essentials, ready for a test drive in the real world.

The goal is to validate your business assumptions, gather feedback on your product's core offerings, and improve it over time.

Building an MVP aligns well with the lean startup philosophy, which emphasizes building products with a ready market in mind.

Why is an MVP so critical for startups, you ask? Well, here are some key reasons:

Risk Mitigation: An MVP lets you test your concept with the least amount of investment, reducing the risk of launching a product no one wants.

Customer Feedback: With an MVP, you collect invaluable user insights to make data-driven improvements.

Time-Efficient: The quicker you launch, the faster you can make updates based on real-world usage.

Cost-Effective: Let's face it, Money is often tight in the startup world. An MVP allows you to go live without draining your funds.

Attracts Investors: An MVP isn't just a basic product; it's proof of concept that can help you attract serious investment without complex arrangements.

Now, not all startup founders opt for an MVP. Some may choose a prototype or a proof of concept to pitch to investors.

Others might aim for a minimum marketable product that offers more than just the bare essentials, especially if breaking into a competitive market.

But if you're looking to validate your business idea quickly and efficiently, an MVP is often your best bet.

And as for how Bubble makes this easier? Well, that's a topic for our next section!

Apps Built On Bubble: MVP Case Studies

Case Study 1: How CircleHome Built a Thriving Business in Under a Year with €120K[b] in Funding

CircleHome, a Spanish startup, aims to make travel more affordable through a home-swapping service. In less than a year, founders David Liria and Xavier Lloveras raised €120,000 in pre-seed funding and amassed 60,000 potential users—all without spending on marketing.

Origin and MVP Approach

David and Xavier initially brainstormed CircleHome as a barter-driven alternative to Airbnb during their business school capstone project. Recognizing the idea's potential, they decided to turn it into a full-scale business.

Lacking technical expertise, they opted for Bubble, a no-code platform, to bring their idea to life quickly. Within four months, they developed a basic but functional version of their platform, validating the MVP model's effectiveness in rapid prototyping and market testing.

Bubble's Role

The key Bubble feature that set them apart was its full-stack capability. Unlike traditional website builders, Bubble offered a complete backend database, allowing the non-technical founders to handle everything from use[c]r profiles to a subscription-based revenue model.

This empowered David and Xavier to build and refine their product without having to learn coding, thereby accelerating their time-to-market.

Growth and Funding:

This MVP was compelling enough to earn them a spot in Startupbootcamp, one of Europe’s biggest startup accelerators.

Shortly after, they secured €120,000 in pre-seed funding. With funds in hand, the startup plans to ramp up user acquisition and expand its business globally.

Key Takeaways

CircleHome's journey exemplifies the power of the MVP model for rapid development and market validation.

Bubble’s all-in-one backend and database functionalities allowed the founders to make this vision a reality, even with no technical background.

CircleHome's growth strategy is now poised to expand beyond Europe, targeting younger users between ages 25-45 in major cities worldwide.

The core of CircleHome's success lies in leveraging Bubble's full-stack capabilities and adhering to MVP principles for quick and effective market entry.

Ownify turned to Bubble, a no-code platform, to build their MVP. Here's why:

Time Savings

With Bubble, Ownify was able to go from concept to a working MVP in record time. The drag-and-drop interface allowed them to focus on the business logic rather than getting bogged down by coding.

Cost-Efficiency

Bubble offered a cost-effective solution that fit well within Ownify's budget constraints. The platform's pricing model allowed them to start small and scale as needed.

Speed

Bubble's robust architecture ensured that Ownify's MVP ran smoothly, handling user queries and transactions at high speed.

Role of EPYC

EPYC played a crucial role in helping Ownify build an admin portal and a user-friendly user portal. These portals became the backbone of Ownify's operations, streamlining administrative tasks and enhancing user experience.

Integrated Technologies

To make their MVP more powerful, Ownify integrated various third-party services:

  • Microsoft Clarity for Analytics: To track user behavior and improve UX.

  • Intercom: For real-time customer support.

  • Google Maps API: To provide location-based services.

  • Stripe: For secure payment gateways.

  • Calculator: To help users calculate their rent-like payments.

  • Send it for Email Automation: To keep users engaged and informed.

  • Hubspot for CRM: To manage customer relationships effectively.

  • Typeform: For interactive forms and surveys.

The Funding Milestone

Armed with a strong MVP, Ownify was able to secure $7-8 million in funding. This financial backing has enabled them to scale their operations and reach a wider audience.

Ownify's journey is a testament to the power of choosing the right platform for building an MVP.

Bubble's time and cost efficiencies were instrumental in helping Ownify raise millions in funding and make strides in the real estate marketing sector.

Key Takeaways

Bubble offers a fast, cost-effective way to build MVPs.

Integration with third-party services can add immense value to your product.

A strong MVP can be a game-changer when it comes to securing funding.

Title: Glide vs. Adalo: A Thorough Comparison of Speed, Design, and Functionality
Author: team-epyc
Content:
In the world of no-code app-building platforms, Glide and Adalo are two names that stand out of the herd.

Both platforms flaunt unique features that cater to a wide array of users, from non-tech professionals to seasoned developers.

But how do these platforms perform in real-world scenarios?

This blog post comprehensively compares Glide and Adalo in terms of speed, design, and functionality

Whether you are an individual, a developer, or a business owner, our objective is to guide you in selecting the right platform for your project needs.

Glide vs. Adalo – which edges out the other, Let’s find out!

Let’s glide through GLIDE

Glide is a no-code app builder; designed to help businesses create powerful and beautiful apps from spreadsheets, usually in just minutes.

What sets Glide apart is its:

  1. User-friendly interface

  2. Drag-and-drop adjustments

  3. Real-time data syncing

It takes data from Google Sheets, Excel, Airtable, and more, turning it into versatile mobile and web apps.

Whether you're well-versed in app development or a beginner exploring options, Glide's robust template library could be a key asset in your toolkit.

Glide’s an ideal choice, particularly if you're looking to get your app or MVP up and running quickly!

What is Adalo?

Just like Glide, Adalo is another prominent no-code app builder renowned for its ability to create both Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) and native applications, offering a blend of functionality and flexibility.

Adalo lets users,

  1. Add customized databases.

  2. Incorporate external data.

  3. User-friendly yet comprehensive interface.

The platform is ideal for various audiences ranging from small businesses to larger organizations.

While it provides robust features, the learning curve and build speed may require some consideration.

Thus, If you're looking for a tool that bridges the gap between PWAs and native apps and offers extensive customization, Adalo might fit your needs, keeping in mind the potential challenges in the learning process and development pace.

Speed – How Fast Can You Build?

The comparison of Glide vs. Adalo in the context of speed reveals some vital differences:

Glide:

  • Instant Setup: With Glide, you can create a simple app within a few hours. Its interface is user-friendly, making it perfect for quick prototyping.

  • Pre-made Templates: Glide offers various templates to speed up development. It’s the right choice for those looking to launch a minimal app quickly.

Adalo:

  • Robust Customization: Adalo’s platform allows for a more extensive degree of customization

  • Although this leads to more prolonged development, the end product can be tailored to specific needs.

  • Learning Curve: Adalo's power comes at the cost of a steeper learning curve, which might slow down the initial progress.

Summary:

In the speed department, Glide leads with its quick setup and ease of use, whereas Adalo takes more time but offers more depth.

Design Aspects – A Closer Look at Aesthetics

Designing an app that resonates with users is crucial, and both Glide and Adalo have their strengths and weaknesses.

Glide:

  • Simplicity and Elegance: Glide's design options are straightforward, enabling even beginners to craft visually pleasing apps.

  • Limited Customization: While user-friendly, Glide’s design capabilities may be restrictive for seasoned designers seeking intricate designs.

Adalo:

  • Rich Design Options: Adalo offers extensive design control, enabling professional-looking applications that align with your brand.

  • Time Consumption: Greater control means more time spent perfecting the design, which may extend development time.

Summary:

For simple and elegant designs, Glide is a go-to option. Adalo, on the other hand, is suitable for those who want a high level of control over their app's appearance.

Functionality – What Can Your App Do?

An app’s worth is often judged by its functionality. Here’s a comparison of Glide vs. Adalo in this context:

Glide:

  • Easy Data Display: Ideal for applications that need to showcase information. Connecting to Google Sheets is a breeze.

  • Limited Complexity: While excellent for basic functionality, Glide might not be suitable for more complex app requirements.

Adalo:

  • Complex Actions and Integrations: Adalo shines with its ability to create multifunctional apps. Its integration with various APIs adds to its capabilities.

  • Potential Performance Issues: More complexity might lead to performance challenges that require careful consideration during the build process.

Summary:

If your focus is on functionality, Glide is best for simpler apps, while Adalo caters to more complex requirements.

Integration with Other Tools

Integration with other tools can enhance an app’s capabilities. Here's how Glide vs. Adalo fares in this area:

Glide:

  • Smooth Integration with Google Sheets: Ideal for those already using Google Workspace.

  • Limited Third-party Integrations: While smooth with Google tools, Glide may have limitations with other third-party tools.

Adalo:

  • Extensive Third-party Integrations: Adalo allows for integration with various tools through APIs, expanding its functionality.

Summary:

Glide's integration is simple and intuitive, especially with Google Sheets, while Adalo provides a broader spectrum of integration options.

Community Support and Documentation

Glide:

  • Community Support: An active community of developers and enthusiasts to assist with queries.

  • Clear Documentation: Well-written guides for beginners.

Adalo:

  • Diverse Community: A rich community of professionals and hobbyists.

  • Extensive Documentation: Detailed tutorials and guides for all levels of developers.

Summary:

Both Glide and Adalo are backed by strong communities and documentation, providing ample support for developers.

Glide vs. Adalo: Commercial Aspect and Pricing Comparison

Considering the financial aspect, both platforms offer different pricing strategies, and your choice might depend on your budget and required features.

Adalo:

  • Free Plan: Unlimited apps, Unlimited screens, Adalo branding, up to 50 rows of data.

  • Paid Plans: $36/month to $200/month for custom domains, Apple and Google Store publishing, connecting to external data collections, 500,000+ monthly app actions, additional data storage.

Glide:

  • Free Basic Plan: Up to 500 rows of data, 100 updates/month, public sign-in, and 3 applications at max.

  • Paid Plans: $25/month to $249/month for custom domains, up to 100,000 data rows, no Glide branding, lower transaction fees, 100+ private users, and more features.

Summary:

Adalo pricing is suitable for users needing extensive customization and app-publishing options.

Glide's pricing is more specific and is best for those focusing on data capabilities and seeking fewer transaction fees.

In short, choose Adalo for broader features and Glide for data-focused and budget-friendly customization.

Conclusion

The comparison of Glide vs. Adalo is multifaceted. Glide is a perfect choice for individuals and small businesses looking for quick and simple app development.

Whereas Adalo, with its robust customization and functionality, suits those looking to create more complex and functionality-rich applications.

Ultimately, pick your champion by understanding your specific needs and aligning them with what each platform excels at.

By understanding the differences in,

  1. Speed,

  2. Design, and,

  3. Functionalities

you can choose the best tool for your unique project.

If you still feel the need to consult an expert or are unsure about going at it alone, feel free to reach us out. 👨🏼‍🏫

We've had our share of experience in shaping state-of-the-art no-code apps, find us right next door.

Title: Seamless WordPress to Webflow Migration: Your Ultimate Guide in 2023
Author: team-epyc
Content:
Is your WordPress website beginning to feel limiting? 

Are you captivated by no-code platforms and the functionalities Webflow offers, but overwhelmed by the thought of migrating from WordPress to Webflow?

Rest easy. As a Webflow development agency, we consider it our duty to address your questions and concerns. 

Sit back and focus on other vital decisions for your business while this comprehensive guide elucidates the reasons and methods for a smooth WordPress to Webflow migration.

Why migrate in the first place?

Without a doubt, WordPress has long been a favored content management system (CMS) for many. 

However, as it has evolved, it hasn't necessarily kept pace with the increasing demand for functionalities. 

It becomes limiting in terms of security, design, customizability, and demands intensive maintenance for larger operations.

Webflow, by contrast, emerges as a state-of-the-art no-code platform. It empowers virtually anyone to craft visually appealing and responsive websites with minimal coding knowledge.

Below are some specific points to help clarify whether a WordPress to Webflow migration aligns with your goals.

Just drag and drop

Unlike WordPress, Webflow boasts a visual drag-and-drop interface. You can view changes in real time, making the platform user-friendly for those without coding experience. 

This is particularly useful for deploying websites with a lean team.

However, with certain frameworks like Elementor, WordPress can facilitate more customizations.

The design freedom you've always craved

Design constraints can be a major downside when using WordPress, and maybe that's why you've landed here.

Webflow lets you craft unique designs that resonate with your brand identity, facilitating an enhanced user experience. 

You can achieve complex designs on WordPress too, but it often necessitates a lot of custom coding, requiring the expertise of a dedicated PHP and JavaScript developer.

Clean code, cleaner websites

Initially, your WordPress site might have showcased cleaner code. However, as you incorporate more plugins, themes, and customizations, the code becomes cluttered and difficult to maintain.

This can adversely affect user experience, performance, security, and scalability. 

Webflow, however, automatically generates neat HTML, CSS, and Javascript code based on your design choices, without depending on third-party plugins, resulting in cleaner and faster-loading websites – crucial for user retention.

Responsive Designs

Your WordPress theme might not be responsive, causing it to look outdated and cluttered on varying screen sizes. 

Transitioning to Webflow ensures that your site looks and performs consistently across devices, The interface is user-friendly and allows for effortless modifications across various device breakpoints. You can swiftly toggle between desktop, tablet, and mobile breakpoints to make adjustments and carry out testing. 

Integrated Hosting and Ecosystem

A WordPress to Webflow migration streamlines your hosting by providing an integrated ecosystem where design, development, and hosting are interconnected. 

This facilitates smoother workflows, one-click deployments and reduces reliance on third-party services.

Enhanced Security 

In WordPress, the plethora of plugins can be a security headache. Each plugin comes from a different developer team, making it hard to maintain consistent security measures across the board. Plus, manual updates for these plugins introduce further vulnerabilities.

Webflow, on the other hand, minimizes these risks. It rarely requires third-party integrations, and when it does, they're from trusted industry leaders. These are vetted and overseen by Webflow's own team, resulting in a far more secure platform.

Step by Step Walkthrough of an Effortless WordPress to Webflow Migration 

Step 1: Your WordPress to Webflow migration voyage should start by crafting an enticing Webflow website. If your needs are modest, a free workspace and CMS website hosting plan should suffice. For more intricate requirements, consider premium plans or the expertise of a specialized Webflow development agency.

Step 2: Evaluate your current website's metrics, such as active users, keyword rankings, conversion rates, and average user duration. This pre-migration assessment facilitates a post-migration comparison (likely the reason for your migration).

Note: Post-migration fluctuations in search rankings are typical. However, a strategic migration approach will expedite recovery.

Step 3: Time to export your content from WordPress. Exporting isn't daunting, but remember: WordPress exports in XML, whereas Webflow requires CSV. Use WordPress plugins that support CSV exports if necessary. At EPYC, we use the WP ALL Export plugin. 

Step 4: In Webflow, create separate collections such as Blogs, Team Members, Podcasts, and so on. Then, for each of these collections, perform a mass import of data from the CSV files that were generated in the preceding step.

Quick guide:

  1. Open the collection panel in Webflow.

  2. Select an existing collection or create a new one.

  3. Click the import button in the collection settings.

  4. Choose the header row.

Note: Webflow permits only 50 collection imports on the free plan. For more, just upgrade to a CMS or Business plan. Also, the maximum CSV file size is 4MB.

Step 5: Implement 301 Redirects. 

After a successful WordPress to Webflow migration, URL changes are probable. Implement 301 redirects (permanent page moves) for altered URLs, ensuring seamless navigation.

On Webflow:

  1. Visit Project settings.

  2. Click the 'Publishing' tab. Scroll down to the '301 redirects' section.

  3. Enter the links in the “Old Path” and “Redirect to Page” fields.

  4. Click “Add Redirect Path”.‍

‍Step 6: Test rigorously. Before going live, assess your website as an average user would, checking for glitches, broken links, and speed issues. 

Tools like Semrush's Site Audit can help pinpoint and fix problems.

Step 7: Migrate your domain. Add your domain to Webflow's settings. Then, modify the DNS settings with your domain provider.

Step 8: Analyze, Optimize & Repeat. Monitor analytics to identify high-performing and lagging pages. 

Enhance the latter and apply successful strategies across your site to ensure an effective WordPress to Webflow migration.

Post Migration Tips

Following the steps above will ensure a successful WordPress to Webflow migration, and now it’s time to let the transition fruit, how? 

Ensure these measures in order to enhance your website's performance post-migration.

  1. Compare pre and post-migration metrics for disparities and errors.

  2. Allow your new site to adapt to Google's algorithm before making substantial content edits.

  3. Integrate Google Analytics and Search Console for user insights and SEO strategies.

  4. Optimize "meta title" and "meta description" tags. Focus on quality keyword-centric content. Compress heavy data like images for improved load times.

Concluding Thoughts

Navigating transitions may seem daunting, yet with careful preparation, they become entirely manageable. This comprehensive guide is designed to streamline your WordPress to Webflow migration.

However, harnessing the full potential of this platform remains entirely within your reach.

As Webflow paves the way for personal and business growth, remember that you don't have to go at it alone. 

At EPYC, we have had our share of experience in making this transition easier for our clients. Therefore, if you feel the need to consult an expert, our team is here to provide guidance and support throughout your migration journey.

Ready to make the switch? Contact us now for a seamless transition and unlock the endless possibilities of Webflow.

Title: How to Choose the Right Webflow Development Agency?
Author: team-epyc
Content:
Are you struggling to find the right Webflow development agency for your business? 

Do you know what your goals are, but are unsure which agency will deliver the best outcomes in line with those goals? If so, this article is for you.

For the past 5 years, the no-code and low-code sector has exploded with innovations, enabling creativity and offering easy-to-use templates. 

The no-code space aims to empower the majority of new startup founders who either can't code or don't have much time to do so. 

And one of the top no-code platforms in this space is Webflow, which is suitable for companies of all shapes and sizes.

You can be stacked with a kickass tech team, but still lack pace in launching and creating websites.

To avoid this, outsourcing all your technical efforts to the right Webflow agency is a significant step to consider. 

It allows you to delegate all the design and development-related strategic work to experts in the field while you focus on a myriad of other important tasks.

We've been in your shoes, and we understand the challenges. 

So, before you start contacting Webflow development agencies, consider all the factors listed below:

Determine your Business Objectives & Budget: 

Be as specific as possible and document everything. Are you planning a complete rebranding, a fresh look, or conversion optimizations? Do you need interactive animations, or do you want a website that can be easily customised by your marketing/sales professionals? 

Also, list all the marketing and tracking tools or softwares you want your agency to integrate. Analyze, how much time can you realistically allocate for website development.

To set up clear grounds for sound budgeting – factor in costs like maintenance and subscriptions, don't hesitate to negotiate with the agency to align with your budget constraints. 

It's wise to include a contingency budget for unexpected expenses and to maintain regular communication with the agency to monitor progress W.R.T spending. By being thorough and transparent, you can find an agency that fits your needs while staying within your budget

Inquire About Their Approach, Pricing Structure, and Working Framework:

Relevance: Have they worked in the same field as yours, or do they cater to some customers in the similar domain.

Process: Differentiate amateurs from experts by their transparency. A reputable Webflow development agency will be clear about their process and approach to development & optimization. Ensure their methods are data-driven and investigate the data they'll gather and their quality testing procedures.

Cost: Find out what's included in their core service and what isn’t. If an agency's charges are considerably lower than others, ask why. They might prioritise revenue over quality.

Organisational Framework: Understand if the agency tends to overcommit to clients or if you'll receive personalised attention. Will you have a dedicated Project Manager? If so, who? Also, check how much experience does the team hold? 

Ask for reference: Request them to connect you with one of their former customers so you can learn about their experience.

Review Their Portfolios & Case Studies: 

Examine testimonials on their website or request case studies if not published. Identify a project similar to yours and ask pertinent questions about it. Don't just consider the quantity; focus on the quality of their work, such as site information architecture, attention to detail, visual structure, load speed, and other functionalities. This will help you shortlist the right Webflow development agency.

You can take a glimpse of our work, which might provide a benchmark for what you could expect. Being vigilant around portfolios, including ours, allows you to make an informed decision, ensuring that the chosen agency has the proven experience and creativity to bring your vision to life.

Assess the Array of Offered Services: 

Opt for Webflow development agencies that offer a wide range of services tailored to your business needs. Typically, this includes Webflow design and development, SEO, migration to Webflow, content creation, website maintenance, and conversion rate optimization. While each agency has its specialties, the services mentioned are fundamental for any top-tier project. Some agencies also specialize in integrations and migrations for a seamless user experience and optimal functionality. Your choice should be based on your vision and the services the agency provides.

Can They Manage Projects Efficiently?: 

A reliable Webflow agency should have proven, efficient procedures in place. Ensure that your project will be completed within the desired timeframe and budget and that the agency can adeptly handle challenges. Confirm that they possess strong project management skills. 

The only way to do this would be, to get in touch with the project manager and understand their mindspace, try and figure what value could they bring onto the project and how well they cope with challenges and timelines.

Do their value systems match yours? 

Matching your values with the agency you want to collaborate with is of paramount importance. 

Aligning values ensures that both your organization and the agency are working towards the same goals and share similar philosophies. This alignment fosters a harmonious working relationship, where communication is more transparent, and misunderstandings are minimized. A shared value system can contribute to the overall success of the project, as it often leads to better collaboration, shared enthusiasm for the work, and a mutual understanding of expectations and standards. Without this alignment, you may encounter conflicts, delays, and a product that doesn't fully meet your vision or values.

It's Time to Decide: 

Engage with your chosen Webflow development agency with a detailed proposal. It should encompass an overview of the project, insight into your company's background, clear objectives with their reasons, a defined project scope (including branding and assets), and crucial timelines. After agreeing on a partnership, provide the agency with brand guidelines, copy, and marketing materials. Get to know the team or project manager handling your project. It's vital for both parties to have a shared understanding and alignment.

We Almost Forgot: Seek What’s Within 

We nearly overlooked that seeking a partner through your network of friends or acquaintances can offer substantial advantages. Leveraging personal connections tends to foster trust and accountability, qualities that are essential in a professional relationship. 

Friends and acquaintances likely have firsthand knowledge of the agency's work ethic, reliability, and quality of work, allowing you to make a more informed decision. 

Conclusion:

None of you would want their hard work to be realised differently. 

When searching for an agency, aim for one that believes in pixel perfection – a partner who's not only technically adept but strategically aligned with your vision. Consider how they fit into your overall project roadmap before taking the next step. 

Remember, choosing the right Webflow development agency isn't just about getting the job done; it's about finding a collaborative ally that can make your website shine and set you apart from the competition. Don't just settle – strive for excellence, and let your website be the game-changer you need!

Great websites result from the collaboration of UI/UX strategists, but developers bring them to life.

If you're seeking an agency passionate about building design-centric websites on Webflow, talk to us.

Title: Webflow vs Bubble: Which No-Code Builder Should you Choose
Author: team-epyc
Content:
It’s 2023 — building websites and applications has never been easier, all thanks to the rapid advancement in no-code development.

Among the frontrunners of this revolution are Webflow and Bubble; breaking the shackles which have kept designers, marketers, business owners and other non-tech enthusiasts distant from the realm of coding.

Having immersed ourselves in no-code tools for almost 3 years now, we frequently encounter a recurring question “Which tool is better, Webflow or Bubble?”

Although both platforms might look similar on the outside, comparing them is like comparing apples to oranges.

Where one platform excels in crafting beautiful yet sophisticated websites, the other can be leveraged into creating highly functional web apps.

Therefore this blog is not your typical “Webflow vs Bubble” propaganda, but a detailed analysis of what each of these platforms have to offer, how they differ, and who they are best suited for.

Intrigued? Let’s dive right in!

First things First

What is Webflow?🤔

Webflow is a no-code website builder which facilitates a seamless blend of design in a visual interface.

Think of it as a middle ground between platforms like Wix and conventional coding.

Where in platforms like wix, you can create simple yet cool websites by dragging and dropping whatever you need wherever you need.

Coding on the other hand can provide infinite depth to your website, but demands precision and bandwidth.

With Webflow, you can just drag and drop to build stunning websites, and if you're ever in a slump, there's always a bunch of awesome templates to choose from. 

But unlike Wix or squarespace, it also allows you to access the code for more complex customizations. 

Although, to pursue these customizations you need to be adept with

  • CSS,

  • Javascript, and

  • HTML

As these are the languages the platform understands and functions around!

You can also create web apps on Webflow, just be ready to compromise on functionalities.

Understanding Bubble?

In layman terms, Bubble is a no-code tool that helps in building intricate digital products, especially web apps. 

But unlike Webflow, which primarily focuses on the frontend. Bubble seamlessly integrates the frontend with a higher degree of logical functionalities and customizations on the backend. 

It empowers users across various industries to construct fully tailored web applications and workflows, spanning from intricate online marketplaces and sophisticated SaaS products to straightforward prototypes.

Given Bubble's focus on complex web projects, it boasts an extensive selection of plugins, themes, templates, and comprehensive database management features.

Webflow vs Bubble: Analysing their purpose and core strengths💪

Webflow shines for its web design capabilities, empowering individuals to manifest their creative visions into live websites without the constraints of coding.

Its real-time visual interface is a game changer, it means that as you design, you're at the same time creating the code (It's like having the power of CSS, HTML and Javascript in a visual editor). 

The platform rocks in facilitating the creation of responsive designs thanks to the flex box responsive engines, ensuring websites maintain their aesthetic and functionality across a spectrum of devices, from desktops to mobiles.

Bubble on the other hand, stands out from traditional no-code builders[a] with its emphasis on web app development.

It boasts a range of interactive tools, enabling the creation of diverse elements, from user-generated content to real-time updates, paving the way for both basic and intricate platforms like marketplaces.

A notable feature of Bubble is its integrated backend, allowing:

  1. Database management

  2. User authentication,

  3. Data tasks

All of this In one unified space, skipping the need for separate backend work and simplifying the app-making journey.

Additionally, Bubble's custom workflows offer the flexibility to define the app's behavior, from basic tasks like user registration to more complex functions, ensuring a tailored user experience.

Here’s a quick comparison 👇 on the basis of feature and functionality

Webflow vs Bubble: Which is easier to Use and is more Customizable

Webflow, with its drag-and-drop feature, can literally cater to anyone and everyone.  

Although if you need to delve deeper into its advanced functionalities, it might present a learning curve.

In terms of customization and flexibility, Webflow stands out for its extensive design adaptability, whether it is about adding interactions or animations; it allows users to modify nearly every facet of their site's look and behavior, but for much intricate practices it requires addition of custom code.

Bubble's visual editor is tailored for web application building, and is welcoming for beginners, but mastering intricate applications demands a grasp of its unique logic and workflows.

While Bubble prioritizes functionality over design, it compensates with its strong backend customization, particularly in crafting custom workflows and actions for web apps. In contrast, Webflow shines in frontend design customization.

Webflow vs Bubble: Plugins and API Integration

Webflow's marketplace is a treasury of third-party integrations and widgets, enabling users to enhance their websites with tools tailored for everything from e-commerce to social media.

While Bubble directly integrates popular plugins like Zapier, Airtable, and Memberstack, Webflow users can achieve similar functionalities by tapping into third-party services available in its marketplace.

Beyond just the marketplace offerings, Webflow's prowess is further accentuated by its robust API capabilities, allowing for a more interconnected web experience by linking with various software tools and databases.

On the other side, Bubble boasts an extensive plugin ecosystem both paid and free, catering to those desiring functionalities from payment gateways to chat features.

Just like Webflow, Bubble also champions seamless API integrations, ensuring users can effortlessly mesh their Bubble-created apps with a wide range of platforms and services, creating a unified digital landscape.

Webflow vs Bubble: Search Engine Optimized?

Webflow stands out with its stupendous SEO capabilities, ensuring that content is primed for search engines right from the get-go.

This built-in feature set, which includes user-friendly tools like meta tags, alt attributes, and automatically generated XML sitemaps, provides a seamless experience for users aiming for search engine visibility.

Another factor that makes Webflow SEO friendly is the loading speed. Websites built on Webflow are quick to load and Google notes it all.

Bubble, while offering foundational SEO settings, requires users to manually build out the UI for blogs and meticulously structure posts for SEO optimization.

The flexibility is there, especially with the ability to integrate plugins or third-party tools for enhanced SEO capabilities, and to set parameters for dynamic content.
However, the ease of achieving this is notably more straightforward in Webflow. In essence, while both platforms have their merits, the potential SEO hit when not using Webflow for landing pages and blog content emerges as a significant downside.

In this context, Bubble's primary challenge, from an SEO perspective, becomes evident.

What’s heavier on the pocket: Webflow vs Bubble💲

When weighing Bubble against Webflow in terms of pricing, both platforms present a tiered approach tailored to diverse needs.

Bubble's free plan is perfect for beginners, offering foundational tools for app development.

As you scale,

  • The starter plan $29/month

  • Growth plan $119

  • Team plan $349 respectively.

For businesses seeking maximum scalability and security, Bubble's Enterprise plan offers custom solutions.

Similar to Bubble, Webflow's Starter is free and ideal for those new to the platform.

  • The Basic plan is priced at $14/month (billed yearly).

  • The CMS plan at $23/month is tailored for content-rich sites,

  • The Business plan at $39/month is optimized for high-traffic marketing sites.

In essence, while Bubble focuses on backend app functionalities across its pricing tiers, Webflow emphasizes frontend design and content management.

Your choice should align with your project's demands and financial considerations.

Creating a No-Code Haven: Combining Webflow and Bubble🤝

What if we said, you could have the best of both worlds?

Combining Webflow and Bubble can be a powerful strategy, leveraging the strengths of both platforms to create a comprehensive web solution.

When you merge the designing features of Webflow with the backend capabilities of Bubble, you get a robust no-code solution that's both visually stunning and functionally rich.

Webflow's frontend design capabilities allow for the creation of intricate, responsive, and SEO-optimized websites. Its drag-and-drop interface and extensive design customization options ensure that the user interface is top-notch.

Alternatively, Bubble's strength lies in its backend functionalities, enabling the development of complex web applications with user authentication, databases, and custom workflows.

By integrating Webflow's frontend with Bubble's backend using third-party tools or direct API connections, you can craft a seamless user experience.

Final Remarks🔍

As no-code gains more significance, both Webflow and Bubble have carved niches for themselves.

Webflow, with its:

  1. Design-centric approach

  2. Seamless blend of aesthetics

  3. Functionalities

Makes it a go-to, for those prioritizing visually stunning websites.

Alternatively, Bubble's strength in:

  1. Backend functionalities

  2. Web application development

Makes it a powerhouse for those looking to build intricate web apps without diving deep into traditional coding.

Each platform has its unique advantages, and the choice ultimately boils down to the specific needs and goals of your project.

As the lines between design and development continue to blur, it's empowering to have tools like Webflow and Bubble at our disposal, enabling us to bring our digital visions to life with greater ease and efficiency.

We hope this article aligns with your quest for clarity on Webflow vs. Bubble.

If you require further insights or expert guidance for your upcoming no-code venture, don't hesitate to reach out. Your success in the no-code realm is just a consultation away.

Title: Webflow or Framer? Finding the Right Tool for Your Website Development
Author: team-epyc
Content:
When it comes to building websites, the debate over which platform to use—like Webflow versus Framer, or even Squarespace versus Wix—is as old as the internet itself. But asking which one is better is like asking, "How far can a thought travel?" 

The truth is, there's no one-size-fits-all answer. It all depends on what you need and what you're trying to achieve.

Whether you're a freelancer aiming to craft sleek websites with Webflow or a designer keen on developing dynamic prototypes with Framer, it's important to know what each platform can offer. 

This blog simplifies the decision-making process by highlighting the strengths and ideal user for each tool. 

If you're deciding between Webflow's ease of use and Framer's interactive capabilities, understanding their distinct advantages will help you choose the best fit for your web design journey.

Webflow and Framer: Tools of the Trade

Think of Webflow and Framer as different types of hammers in a carpenter's toolkit. The type of hammer doesn't limit the carpenter's ability to build; rather, it influences the kind of work they can do and the clients they can serve. Similarly, Webflow and Framer are tools that help you build websites, but the choice between them should be based on your project's needs, your skills, and your goals.

Webflow: The Designer's Dream

Webflow is a powerful platform that lets you design, build, and launch responsive websites visually, without needing to write code. It's like having a canvas where you can bring your website ideas to life exactly as you envision them. Webflow is particularly useful for creating professional-looking websites with complex layouts and animations. Plus, it has a workaround for almost everything, making it a versatile choice for most web projects.

Framer: The Interactive Innovator

Framer, on the other hand, is for those who want to dive deep into creating highly interactive and animated websites. Framer is a platform for web design that doesn't require coding, designed to assist in developing prototypes for apps or websites. It offers an environment similar to Figma or Sketch, allowing you to transform your designs into code. Additionally, it supports integration with software such as Photoshop and Figma, making it straightforward to import your work.

Webflow & Framer’s Pros and Cons at a Glance

To simplify your decision, here's a quick overview of the pros and cons of Webflow and Framer:

Who Should Use Webflow and Who Should Use Framer?

Webflow and Framer are both awesome tools, but they're made for different kinds of work. Let's break it down to see which one might be right for you.

Quick Glance at Each User-Base

Webflow: Perfect for Website Designers and Developers

If you're looking to do freelance work making websites, Webflow is your go-to. It's really popular in the industry because it's easy to use and has lots of templates and tools to help you start and finish projects. Webflow is always getting better, adding new features and updates to make your work easier and more creative.

Framer: A Dream for Interactive Prototypes

On the flip side, if you love making things move and interact, like in apps or digital products, Framer is the tool you might want to dive into. It's all about creating cool animations and interactions that can make a prototype feel real. Framer might not be as common as Webflow for freelance work, but it's unbeatable for making detailed, interactive designs.

Choosing What's Right for You

In the end, picking between Webflow and Framer depends on what you want to do and what you're good at. Webflow is great if you're into designing and building websites, especially if you want to work as a freelancer. It's user-friendly and packed with resources to help you get going.

Framer, though, is your buddy if you're into crafting interactive prototypes with lots of animations. It's perfect for projects that need that extra bit of interaction and life.

Both Webflow and Framer have their own pros and cons, so think about what you need for your projects. No matter which one you choose, you're on your way to creating amazing things. 

Our Advice

After evaluating our own needs and experimenting with different platforms, we've chosen to stick with Webflow for most of our projects because it aligns well with what we want to achieve for our clients. 

However, for ecommerce sites, we consider other options like Shopify or WooCommerce, depending on the client's specific needs. Webflow's ecommerce capabilities aren't fully mature yet, so it's about finding the right tool for the job.

Final Thoughts

There's no universally correct answer when choosing between Webflow and Framer. It all comes down to your personal or business needs, the type of websites you're building, and the experiences you want to create. 

Start with a clear plan, list out your requirements, and choose the platform that best fits your vision. 

And remember, nothing is set in stone. As your needs evolve, you can always reassess and switch tools if necessary. After all, they're just tools to help you build your vision.

That being said, if you’re looking for an agency to partner with on your journey to build your beautifully designed & functional website, feel free to reach out to us!

Title: Wait, what is No-Code?
Author: team-epyc
Content:
“The future of coding is no coding at all.” — Chris Wanstrath, GitHub Co-founder.

Remember the personal computers from the early ’80s? You probably don’t, so here’s a picture.

These machines were a nightmare to use because they didn’t have a Graphical User Interface or GUI. Everything was done by writing lines of code.

Anyone born in the late ’90s & after, think of it like not being able to browse through Netflix because you don’t know how to code.

Sounds dystopian, right?

There’s a similar thing going on today, but with Software Development.

There are ~4.2 billion internet users worldwide, but only 19 million software developers (according to a recent Slashdata report).

For every 221 internet users, only one knows how to code.

If one of the other 220 wants to start something online (eCommerce store, SaaS, etc.), they need to spend thousands of dollars on hiring a developer or spending months/years learning how to code.

Enter No-Code.

So, what is No-Code all about?

Simply put, no-code is a way to build web & mobile apps without writing code.

Instead of building your layout with lines of code, you drag & drop elements (buttons, form fields, etc.) to visually build your app.

Want an example?

Here’s our landing page in Visual Studio Code.

& here’s our landing page in Webflow.

We dragged & dropped elements, added a bit of customization & that’s it.

And if you look closely in the first screenshot the code was exported after building the landing page visually.

That’s no-code.

Wait, what is Low-Code then?

The main promise of no-code is to build anything you can imagine without writing a single line of code.

But let’s be real, that’s marketing bullshit.

Everything has its limits. Sometimes the limits are higher, but limits nonetheless.

Low-code is your way to bypass those limits.

You build most of your app without code, & then only write code to build functionalities that weren’t possible earlier. In most cases, you won’t have to leave the platform or set up a separate development environment to do this.

So, can we build anything with No-Code?

Technically, yes. But there are certain limitations.

Most platforms specialize in building something specific. You can build a web, mobile, or even a voice app without code but there are different platforms for each.

Once you figure out what you want to build (example — a web/mobile app or something in AR/VR), you get a couple of platform options to chose from.

We recently published an ebook to kickstart your journey with no-code tools to build apps, games, chatbots & more — download it for free.

Before you go, there’s more coming to this article — pros & cons, use cases & right time to use no-code & how to get started with it.

Follow us on Twitter, LinkedIn & Instagram to stay in the loop! ⚡

Title: Supercharge Your No-Code Apps with 17 Bubble.io Plugins
Author: team-epyc
Content:
Learn all about popular Bubble plugins that make your life easy.

Building your own website or app without writing a single line of code sounds cool, right? 

That's what Bubble.io lets you do! It's like Lego for web development; you just click pieces together. But what makes Bubble.io even more awesome are its plugins. 

Think of plugins as special Lego pieces that let your creation do cool stuff like send texts or look fancy.

What Are Bubble.io Plugins?

Plugins are like power-ups for your Bubble.io projects. They let you add extra features without needing to code. Want to connect to other apps, make your site look cooler, or even send messages? There's a plugin for that!

A Quick Look Back

Bubble.io hasn't always had all these cool plugins. At first, only the Bubble.io team made them. But then, they let anyone create plugins. This was a game-changer! Now, there are tons of plugins because smart people from all over the world are making them.

Picking the Perfect Plugin

With so many plugins out there, how do you choose? Here are some tips:

  • Compatibility: Make sure the plugin works with your Bubble.io version.

  • Instructions: Look for plugins with clear guides on how to use them.

  • Helpful Community: Check if there's a group of users who can help if you get stuck.

  • Fast and Smooth: Make sure the plugin won't slow down your app.

  • Cost: Some plugins are free, and some cost money. Decide what's worth it for your project.

Categories

Bubble sorts plugins into various categories, with many plugins fitting into multiple categories. Here's a quick overview of some popular ones:

  1. Media: Add photos, videos, and animations easily.

  2. Location: Bring maps, distance calculations, and time zones into your app.

  3. Social Network: Integrate social media platforms, like embedding YouTube playlists or linking Discord channels.

  4. Payment: Enhance your app with payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe.

  5. Data (Things): Manage, store, and navigate your data with ease.

Quick Overview of the Top 16 Bubble Plugins

The Best 16 Bubble.io Plugins for 2024

Let's check out some cool plugins that can make your Bubble.io projects even better:

1. Bubble to Airtable Two-way Sync

Category: Data

Price: $25/month

Key Features:

  • Seamless synchronisation between Bubble and Airtable databases.

  • Easy data management without dealing with Airtable's API.

  • Supports bidirectional data flow, ensuring consistency across platforms.

Brief About: This plugin bridges the gap between Bubble's dynamic app development environment and Airtable's user-friendly database management, making it ideal for projects that require robust data handling capabilities.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Install the plugin through the Bubble plugin marketplace.

  • Enter your Airtable API key and Base ID in the plugin settings.

  • Configure the data sync options according to your needs.

2. Stripe Customer Portal

Category: Payment

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Secure customer portal hosted by Stripe on Bubble.io.

  • Allows customers to manage subscriptions and billing details.

  • Customizable to match your branding and business style.

Brief About: This plugin integrates Stripe's powerful payment infrastructure into your Bubble app, offering a seamless experience for managing subscriptions and payments.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Add the plugin to your Bubble app from the marketplace.

  • Connect your Stripe account by entering your Stripe API keys.

  • Customise the portal settings to fit your app's design and requirements.

3. Dynamic Loading Animation Overlay

Category: Media

Price: $6/month or $15 one-time

Key Features:

  • 8 customizable loading animations.

  • Full-screen or element-specific overlay options.

  • Customizable colours and optional text or image overlays.

Brief About: Enhance user experience during load times with attractive animations, keeping users engaged and informed.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Install the plugin and drag the animation element to your page.

  • Choose an animation, customise its appearance, and set its conditions.

  • Apply the overlay to specific elements or as a full-screen overlay.

4. "Mapbox" Maps

Category: Location

Price: $10/month or $45 one-time

Key Features:

  • Customizable MapBox-based maps.

  • Add and manage markers, display 3D buildings, and interact with map clicks.

Brief About: This plugin allows for the integration of detailed and interactive maps into your Bubble app, perfect for location-based services.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • After adding the plugin, place the Mapbox element on your page.

  • Configure your Mapbox API key and customise the map's appearance.

  • Use dynamic data to add markers and define map interactions.

5. Chart JS – Charts and Graphs

Category: Media

Price: $9/month or $195 one-time

Key Features:

  • 24 types of customizable charts.

  • Easy integration with Bubble data for real-time analytics.

  • Comprehensive customization options for design and interaction.

Brief About: Visualise data beautifully with a wide range of chart options, enhancing your app's analytics and reporting capabilities.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Incorporate the plugin from the marketplace into your app.

  • Select the chart type and link it to your Bubble data sources.

  • Customise the chart's appearance and settings to match your app's design.

6. PDF Conjurer

Category: Media

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Generate downloadable, openable, or printable PDFs.

  • Works in both browser and backend workflows.

  • No external services or API required.

Brief About: Create dynamic PDF documents directly within your Bubble app, offering a versatile solution for reports, invoices, and more.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

7. Device and IP Address Data + Map

Category: Data

Price: $5/month or $19 one-time

Key Features:

  • Collects 20 different parameters of app visitors.

  • New map feature to show approximate user locations.

Brief About: Gain insights into your users' devices and locations, enhancing your analytics and personalising the user experience.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Implement the plugin from the Bubble marketplace.

8. Spin to Win Wheel

Category: Media

Key Features:

9. Base64 File Uploader & Downloader

11. CSS Loading Animations

12. Bubble Data API

Category: Data

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Supports all data types for versatile app development.

Brief About: This plugin is a powerhouse for apps requiring deep data manipulation or integration capabilities, making backend processing seamless.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Incorporate the plugin into your Bubble app.

  • Utilise the Data API actions in your workflows to interact with external services or manage data.

13. Fully Customizable Context Menu

Category: Media

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Personalise user interface with customizable context menus.

  • Quick access to common actions enhances user experience.

Brief About: Transform your app's interactivity with tailored context menus, perfect for apps needing dynamic user interaction.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Add the plugin to your project.

  • Configure the context menu options and apply them to the desired elements.

14. Google Docs Plugin

Category: Data

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Streamlines document creation and editing within apps.

  • Facilitates easy collaboration and sharing.

Brief About: This plugin simplifies report generation, invoicing, and document collaboration directly within your Bubble app.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

  • Install the plugin and link your Google account.

  • Set up document creation and editing functionalities in your app workflows.

15. OpenAI Whisper

Category: Data

Price: Free

Key Features:

  • Transcribes and translates audio files accurately.

  • Supports a broad range of languages and audio formats.

Brief About: Perfect for apps requiring voice-to-text features, this plugin leverages OpenAI's Whisper model for seamless audio processing.

Mini-Guide to Setup:

16. Lottie

Category: Media

Price: Free

Key Features:

Brief About: Bring your app to life with smooth, vector-based animations using Lottie, ideal for adding a professional touch to your UI.
